description_long: This is a large wooden half-pipe skate ramp, commonly found in urban parks or recreational areas. The structure features two steep curved ramps on either side, connected by a flat central platform, and is surrounded by metal safety railings on the upper edges. There is a staircase with a landing platform on one end, providing access to the top of the ramp. The surface is decorated with graffiti, including a large hand sign and various tags, giving it an authentic urban feel. The wood shows signs of wear and use, with visible scuff marks and discoloration in the skating areas. The overall construction appears robust, designed to withstand heavy use by skateboarders, BMX riders, and other extreme sports enthusiasts.
description: A large wooden half-pipe skate ramp with metal railings, graffiti art, and a staircase for access. The structure is robust and designed for skateboarding and similar activities.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the half-pipe, graffiti art, and the staircase with railings on the left.
description_view_1: Front view into the curved ramp, highlighting the interior skating surface and a large hand sign graphic.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the top platform, hand sign graffiti, and the staircase with a target graphic on the side.
description_view_3: Opposite front view into the ramp, showing the other side's curve, a pointing hand graphic, and the staircase structure.
